heading: Education and training opportunities for Coast Guard spouses
label: 2904 Education and training opportunities for Coast Guard spouses
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 2904 was renumbered .section 5104 of this title

A, title V, § 580G133 Stat. 1410Dec. 20, 2019“The spouse of a member of the Coast Guard may participate in the My Career Advancement Account program of the Department of Defense if the Coast Guard reimburses the Department of Defense.”
amendments: Amendments2021—Subsec. (b)(1)(B). substituted “an individual” for “a person” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 116–2832018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282section 542 of this title

A, title V, § 580G133 Stat. 1410Dec. 20, 2019“The spouse of a member of the Coast Guard may participate in the My Career Advancement Account program of the Department of Defense if the Coast Guard reimburses the Department of Defense.”

children.text: education and training required for a degree or credential at an accredited college, university, or technical school in the United States that expands employment and portable career opportunities for the spouse; or

education prerequisites and a professional license or credential required, by a government or government-sanctioned licensing body, for an occupation that expands employment and portable career opportunities for the spouse.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term “eligible spouse” means the spouse of a member of the Coast Guard who is serving on active duty and includes a spouse who receives transitional compensation under .section 1059 of title 10

children.label: i
children.text: is married to, but legally separated from, a member of the Coast Guard under a court order or statute of any State or territorial possession of the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is eligible for tuition assistance as a member of the Armed Forces.

children.heading: .—Portable career
children.label: 2 .—Portable career
children.text: The term “portable career” includes an occupation that requires education, training, or both that results in a credential that is recognized by an industry, profession, or specific type of business.
